# 6 benefits of AI in financial services

![6 Benefits of AI in Financial Services](https://www.ocrolus.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/6-Benefits-of-AI-in-Financial-Services-1024x619.jpg)

The financial sector has always been at the forefront of adopting new technology – and the rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no exception.

As the technology continues to grow in popularity across practically every industry, the financial services sector is making great strides in leveraging AI to transform its operations – and is uniquely positioned to benefit from its intelligent application.

While AI has historically been an incredibly powerful tool in the financial sector, recent advancements can revolutionize the industry, enabling financial institutions, including banks and lenders, the opportunity to streamline processes, improve decision-making and enhance the customer experience.

However, AI still has its limitations. It’s not a standalone tool – and its implementation can pose significant challenges for financial institutions without proper oversight. To unlock the productivity and cost-saving benefits AI offers, financial institutions need to go beyond the hype to understand how the technology has evolved and [where human involvement is key](https://www.yoursonar.com/blog/article/human-role-in-ai-driven-mortgage-origination) to ensure accuracy.

## The rise in AI in financial services

The rise of AI in financial services has been driven by several factors, principally cost and time savings.

Many financial institutions are leveraging AI-powered technology to automate and streamline previously time-intensive, manual processes such as [fraud detection](https://www.ocrolus.com/fraud-detection/), risk management and customer service to improve efficiency and reduce operational costs. As a result, many financial firms have turned to AI to help them stay competitive and achieve greater profitability.

![How to Spot Fraud backlink banner](https://www.ocrolus.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/How-to-Spot-Fraud-backlink-banner.webp)

While AI is not new to the financial sector, recent advancements and growing hype surrounding emerging technologies like ChatGPT, Bard and general GPT inspire forward-thinking financial institutions to explore new ways to apply the technology. Plus, the massive amount of data generated by financial transactions provides a [perfect environment for AI to thrive](https://www.ocrolus.com/blog/the-role-humans-with-ai-document-automation/), making it a natural fit for expansion in the financial sector.

On the other hand, businesses and financial services providers that have not yet implemented AI may be at a competitive disadvantage – and risk losing out on its benefits. With its ability to [automate and optimize processes](https://www.ocrolus.com/business-process-automation/), AI can enable financial institutions to provide better, more personalized customer experiences, improve decision-making and reduce operational costs. Failure to keep up can lead to missed opportunities, inefficient processes and lost revenue.

## How AI is used to save time and costs in financial services

Artificial Intelligence is being practically applied in financial services in a variety of ways, from risk management and fraud detection to customer service and trading analysis. One area where AI is particularly useful is in [intelligent document processing](https://www.ocrolus.com/intelligent-document-processing/) for banks and lenders.

AI-powered document automation and processing technology can help financial institutions streamline their operations by automating repetitive manual tasks such as data entry, document sorting and verification. By using AI to process documents, financial institutions can increase efficiency, reduce processing time and minimize errors.

## Top Advantages of Using AI in Financial Services

AI offers several key benefits in financial services, including improved operations, reduced costs, enhanced fraud detection, automated regulatory compliance, reduced risk and faster decision making. By leveraging the power of AI, financial institutions can gain a competitive edge, achieve operational efficiencies and make more informed decisions in a rapidly evolving financial landscape.

#### 1. Improved operations

AI technologies can optimize and automate various processes in financial services, leading to improved operational efficiency. For example, using AI-powered document automation can foster [greater digital collaboration](https://www.ocrolus.com/blog/automation-help-support-remote-workforces/), streamline data entry and document verification tasks as well as reduce manual errors and processing time. AI can also enhance customer service through chatbots and virtual assistants, providing quick and personalized responses to customer inquiries.

#### 2. Reduced costs

AI can help financial institutions [reduce costs](https://www.ocrolus.com/blog/what-is-document-ocr/) by automating labor-intensive tasks and minimizing manual errors. For instance, automating data entry and document processing tasks can significantly reduce operational costs associated with manual labor.

#### 3. Fraud detection

AI has become a powerful tool in [detecting fraud](https://www.ocrolus.com/free-checklist/7-tips-for-preventing-document-fraud-earlier-and-more-accurately/) in financial services. AI algorithms can analyze vast amounts of data in real-time, identify patterns and detect anomalies that may indicate fraudulent activities. This can help financial institutions in proactively identifying and mitigating potential fraud risks, safeguarding their financial assets and protecting their customers’ data.

#### 4. Improved and automated regulatory compliance

Compliance with regulations and laws is a critical aspect of the financial industry. AI can assist financial institutions in [automating regulatory compliance processes](https://www.ocrolus.com/blog/address-lending-issues-with-mortgage-automation/), ensuring adherence to complex regulations and reducing the risk of non-compliance. For example, AI-powered systems can analyze and monitor transactions, customer data and other relevant data to identify compliance risks and flag potential violations.

#### 5. Reduced risk

AI can help financial institutions manage and mitigate risks associated with investments, loans and other financial activities. AI-powered risk management systems can analyze historical data, market trends and other factors to assess risks and make data-driven decisions. This can help financial institutions in [minimizing risks](https://www.ocrolus.com/cash-flow-analysis/#scrollId3), optimizing lending decisions and reducing the likelihood of financial losses.

#### 6. Faster decision making

AI can provide real-time data analysis and insights, enabling financial institutions to make quicker and more informed decisions. AI-powered analytics and predictive modeling can help financial institutions assess risks, identify investment opportunities and optimize business strategies. This can result in faster decision making and increased scalability for lenders in a fast-paced financial market.

AI can greatly benefit those in banking and financial services by automating manual processes, reducing errors and improving accuracy. Ocrolus’ solution is a prime example of how AI can be used to streamline processes and make the lending process more efficient, accurate and transparent.

Providing financial institutions an intelligent way to process and analyze large volumes of complex data, Ocrolus’ [advanced AI-driven automation technology](https://www.ocrolus.com/product/) has the potential to revolutionize the way financial institutions operate. Ocrolus has developed a cutting-edge solution that can analyze data quickly and accurately, improving efficiency and reducing errors in the lending process.

[Book your demo](https://www.ocrolus.com/schedule-a-demo/) to discover how Ocrolus’ helps financial services providers efficiently manage risk, avoid fraud and make informed decisions through intelligent document analysis.

--- SOURCE 2: AI in Finance: Top Use Cases and Benefits ---
URL: https://www.ema.co/additional-blogs/addition-blogs/ai-financial-services-use-cases-benefits

SUMMARY:
Ema, a Universal AI employee, helps financial institutions streamline operations, reduce costs, and improve productivity by automating complex

FULL CONTENT:
![logo](/_next/static/media/ema-logo.267c015f.svg)
![banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2Ffaa5189963b4d25b4956470b58259c0675a1435b-1920x1080.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Published by [Vedant Sharma](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vedant-sharma63/) in [Additional Blogs](/additional-blogs/addition-blogs)

![closeIcon](/_next/static/media/linkedin.82ca66cd.svg)

Copy Link

Financial institutions worldwide are witnessing a fundamental transformation through the implementation of artificial intelligence. What began as experimental technology has evolved into mission-critical infrastructure that drives competitive advantage and operational excellence.

The numbers speak volumes: [AI adoption in finance](https://www.ema.co/additional-blogs/addition-blogs/understanding-the-role-of-ai-in-revolutionizing-the-fintech-industry) has surged to [85%](https://softwareoasis.com/rise-of-ai-in-financial-services-trends-stats/). This acceleration isn't coincidental; it reflects the measurable value that AI for financial services delivers across multiple operational areas.

This blog will explore the top use cases of AI in financial services, examining how it enhances business operations, reduces risks, and offers unprecedented opportunities for revenue growth.

## TL;DR

## AI for Financial Services: Adoption Trends and Market Landscape

AI has shifted from peripheral technology to a foundational driver of business value in financial services. The industry leads most sectors in terms of AI investment: financial institutions spent [$35 billion](https://www.forbes.com/sites/davidparker/2024/10/03/the-future-of-ai-in-financial-services/) on AI in 2023, with forecasts projecting this will reach [$97 billion](https://www.forbes.com/sites/davidparker/2024/10/03/the-future-of-ai-in-financial-services/) by 2027. This makes financial services one of the most invested industries in AI, spanning banking, insurance, capital markets, and payments.

In many organizations, over a third of core processes have high potential for automation or augmentation with AI, underlining a strong commitment to transforming legacy operations into digital-first models.

Suggested Watch: And as you get into the shift, here is a video by the BCG group worth watching:

[The Present and Future of AI in Financial Services](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=epL_L7gWzgM&pp=ygUZQUkgZm9yIEZpbmFuY2lhbCBTZXJ2aWNlcw%3D%3D)

### From Efficiency to Growth and Resilience

The initial focus of AI adoption was rapid cost reduction and operational efficiency, which were near-term metrics that delivered tangible ROI. Today, industry leaders are turning their attention to long-term, strategic outcomes.

### Regulatory and Strategic Imperatives

AI is reshaping value chains, market structures, and competitive dynamics. Early and strategic adoption is quickly becoming the differentiator between industry leaders and laggards.

In fact, as per KPMG's Global head of AI and Tax & Legal, Companies that haven't started on the AI journey or are in the very early stages have a common blocker of ROI. However, the research shows that the ROI is there and significant.

![Hero Banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2F1038709fa6191d10887fb7337ed6c90aca8e445c-695x294.png&w=1920&q=75)

source: <https://assets.kpmg.com/content/dam/kpmg/au/pdf/2025/kpmg-global-ai-in-finance.pdf>

## Top Use Cases of AI in Financial Services

![Hero Banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2Fdebbbedb94fe9a8e2aeb646e406ea7dc66a0e2af-1010x611.png&w=2048&q=75)

[Source: https://www.oecd.org/en/topics/sub-issues/digital-finance/artificial-intelligence-in-finance.html](https://www.oecd.org/en/topics/sub-issues/digital-finance/artificial-intelligence-in-finance.html)

Artificial intelligence is no longer confined to pilot projects or a single department. It actively reshapes core financial services functions, delivering measurable benefits in efficiency, risk management, and customer engagement.

Below are the top AI use cases decision-makers should prioritize, with key statistics underscoring their impact.

### Customer Service Enhancement

Delivering exceptional customer service is paramount in the financial services industry, and AI is revolutionizing this area. AI-powered solutions like chatbots and virtual assistants are becoming integral to customer interactions.

For example, Bank of America’s AI-driven chatbot, [Erica](https://newsroom.bankofamerica.com/content/newsroom/press-releases/2023/07/bofa-s-erica-surpasses-1-5-billion-client-interactions--totaling.html), has been a key tool in helping clients manage their finances. Since its launch in 2018, Erica has surpassed 1.5 billion interactions, with over 37 million clients using the service. In 2023 alone, clients engaged with Erica more than 333 million times, marking a 35% increase year-over-year. This highlights the scalability and efficiency of AI in handling large volumes of customer interactions.

Moreover, AI systems like Erica can assist human agents by providing real-time data and insights, enabling them to deliver more accurate responses. This approach not only improves customer satisfaction but also reduces operational costs. With AI chatbots expected to handle 80% of customer interactions in banking, they are helping to reduce response times by more than 50%.

### Fraud Detection and Prevention

Fraud remains a significant challenge in financial services, but AI is proving to be a game-changer in combating it. AI’s ability to process vast amounts of data in real-time allows it to detect suspicious activities and fraudulent transactions quickly.

A prime example of this is [HSBC](https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/financial-services/how-hsbc-fights-money-launderers-with-artificial-intelligence), which has implemented AI and machine learning to monitor customer transactions for anomalies. The bank has reported a 50% reduction in false positives within its fraud detection system, improving both operational efficiency and the [customer experience](https://www.ema.co/customer-experience) by minimizing unnecessary alerts and investigations. This not only saves time but also enhances trust with customers, who benefit from more accurate fraud prevention.

AI’s continuous learning capabilities allow systems like HSBC’s to evolve and become more precise over time. As more data flows through, these AI models refine their detection algorithms, ensuring that banks remain one step ahead of increasingly sophisticated cybercriminals.

The success of AI in fraud detection is also reflected in the broader financial services, as NVIDIA's tweet showcases how AI agents are driving improvements in fraud detection, customer service, and overall operational efficiency. Leading institutions such as BlackRock, CapitalOne, and RBC are adopting Agentic AI to enhance productivity, security, and customer experiences.

![Hero Banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2Fd21e41a86f95e0c4bf8644750d3e76071acb93e1-563x546.png&w=1200&q=75)

Source: [X post by NVIDIA AI](https://x.com/NVIDIAAI/status/1948079822154125669)

### Credit Scoring and Risk Management

AI is revolutionizing how financial institutions assess creditworthiness, moving beyond traditional models that rely primarily on historical data. Traditional credit scoring often misses key factors, but AI can analyze a broader range of data, including unstructured data such as transaction histories, social media activity, and more, providing a more comprehensive view of a borrower’s financial behavior.

A prime example of AI’s impact is [Zest AI](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/zest-ais-credit-models-proven-to-increase-loan-approvals-for-every-protected-class-302048828.html), a leader in AI-automated underwriting. Over the past four years, Zest AI’s technology has helped lenders increase loan approvals by 49% for Latinos, 41% for Black applicants, 40% for women, 36% for elderly applicants, and 31% for AAPI applicants, all while maintaining consistent risk levels. This demonstrates how AI-driven credit models can promote more inclusive lending practices without compromising risk management. To date, Zest AI has processed over 39 million loan applications, resulting in $250 billion in loans granted.

Zest AI’s models have proven particularly beneficial in increasing access to credit for underrepresented groups, providing a fairer and more equitable approach to lending. The company's AI technology assesses 98% of American adults, enabling lenders to make better, data-driven decisions while significantly boosting loan approval rates.

### AI in Wealth and Asset Management

AI is reshaping wealth management by offering more personalized investment strategies and optimizing portfolio management. AI is transforming wealth management by enhancing portfolio optimization, risk management, and personalized investment strategies.

[BlackRock](https://www.klover.ai/blackrock-ai-strategy-analysis-of-dominance-in-asset-management/), the world’s largest asset manager with $11.6 trillion in assets, leverages AI through its Aladdin platform to drive data-driven investment decisions. Aladdin enables BlackRock to optimize portfolios, manage risk, and identify investment opportunities, leading to record net inflows of $641 billion in 2024.

Aladdin's AI capabilities provide predictive analytics that help fund managers make informed decisions, improving returns and minimizing risks. This platform’s vast data ecosystem allows BlackRock to continually refine AI models, ensuring precision in predicting market trends and adjusting strategies accordingly.

In the trading space, AI powers algorithmic trading. Firms like Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley use AI algorithms to execute high-frequency trades, analyzing millions of data points to identify opportunities in real-time. AI trading now accounts for [60-73%](https://www.benzinga.com/general/topics/23/06/32861724/what-percentage-of-stock-trades-are-made-by-bots-and-algorithms) of all equity trades on U.S. exchanges, highlighting AI’s pivotal role in modern trading strategies.

It's the impact of these use cases that is driving more financial institutions, such as banks, to adopt the benefits AI brings to the table. This tweet by AI in Nigeria reflects the same.

![Hero Banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2Fa53cc06d68f22de2bd5e122e29b8da25c243352b-557x451.png&w=1200&q=75)

Source: [X post by Ai In Nigeria](https://x.com/aiinnigeria/status/1947961654651523393)

## Quantifiable Benefits of AI in Finance for Decision Makers

![Hero Banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2F17ea6b5b9e4de509c5c3dc3c08015bb125c43d25-1900x1080.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

[AI adoption delivers concrete, measurable benefits](https://www.ema.co/additional-blogs/addition-blogs/key-applications-and-benefits-of-ai-in-banking) that go beyond just technology adoption. It’s about creating real efficiencies, cutting costs, and uncovering new avenues for revenue growth. For leaders, these outcomes are proving essential in driving smarter strategies and better results

### Operational Efficiency Gains

AI is significantly improving operational efficiency across the financial services sector. Many financial institutions are reporting notable time savings and enhanced performance due to AI-powered automation. This improvement in efficiency directly translates to reduced costs and optimized use of resources.

For example, document processing, which traditionally required hours of manual review, is now completed in minutes with the help of AI-powered optical character recognition (OCR) and natural language processing (NLP). This allows staff to focus on higher-value tasks while streamlining operations.

### Cost Reduction and ROI

The financial impact of AI implementation extends beyond efficiency gains. Cost savings from AI-led fraud detection will increase to [$10.4](https://http://juniperresearch.com/press/ai-enabled-financial-fraud-detection-spend/) billion globally in 2027, according to Juniper Research projections.

Institutions implementing comprehensive AI strategies report substantial reductions in operational expenses, particularly in areas requiring high-volume data processing or repetitive decision-making tasks.

### Revenue Enhancement

Beyond cost savings, AI in financial services creates new revenue opportunities through improved customer acquisition, enhanced product offerings, and optimized pricing strategies. AI-driven insights enable institutions to identify profitable customer segments, develop targeted marketing campaigns, and launch innovative financial products.

Investment management firms using AI for portfolio optimization consistently deliver superior risk-adjusted returns, attracting additional assets under management and increasing fee income.

### Ema’s Role in Enhancing AI Capabilities in Financial Services

Artificial intelligence in financial services is pushing boundaries in ways previously thought unattainable, and Ema—a Universal AI employee—is at the forefront of this transformation. Ema, designed to function across multiple roles within financial institutions, from [customer support](https://www.ema.co/customer-support-assistant) to data analysis and compliance, offers a game-changing approach to automating workflows and enhancing productivity.

#### Why Choose Ema?

Ema is not just another AI tool; it is an [Agentic AI](https://www.ema.co/additional-blogs/addition-blogs/understanding-the-meaning-and-significance-of-agentic-ai) platform capable of performing multiple specialized tasks autonomously. Thanks to its [Generative Workflow Engine](https://www.ema.co/blog/agentic-ai/generative-workflow-engine-building-emas-brain)™, Ema can execute complex workflows, break down tasks into manageable subtasks, and complete them end-to-end without the need for human intervention.

Financial institutions can leverage Ema’s capabilities to streamline operations across departments, improve accuracy, and boost efficiency, all while maintaining compliance with industry standards like SOC 2, ISO 27001, and GDPR.

Ema also integrates seamlessly with over 200 enterprise applications, making it a versatile addition to any organization.

#### How Ema Enhances Key Financial Services Operations

#### TrueLayer Case Study: Revolutionizing Customer Support with Ema

A real-world example of Ema’s effectiveness in financial services can be seen in [TrueLayer](https://www.ema.co/resources/customer/revolutionizing-customer-support), a UK-based fintech pioneer revolutionizing financial services with open banking APIs. TrueLayer needed to enhance its customer support operations to handle complex payment issues more efficiently while automating routine queries.

Ema’s AI-powered Customer Support Assistant seamlessly integrated with TrueLayer’s Zendesk system, automating the resolution of over 82% of customer support cases. By learning from TrueLayer’s knowledge base, Ema quickly adapted to the technical jargon and processes specific to TrueLayer’s products, resolving customer issues with the same level of expertise as a seasoned support agent.

Ema also excels at recognizing when escalation to human agents is required, ensuring a seamless handover for more sensitive cases related to fraud, security, and compliance.

TrueLayer’s Technical Support Lead, Elizaveta Shevchenka, expressed confidence in the partnership, stating, “Every week, Ema's Customer Support Assistant AI Employee kept improving its accuracy and efficiency. The rapid progress is a testament to Ema's unique approach to building Gen AI applications.”

![Hero Banner](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fq7d1vb20%2Fproduction%2F2b5cd4c6e1834b5991a0680dc465c8a49e6b1fca-753x291.png&w=1920&q=75)

## Conclusion

From enhancing customer service to revolutionizing fraud detection, risk management, and lending practices, AI is driving measurable benefits across all areas of finance. Leading institutions like Bank of America, HSBC, and BlackRock are already capitalizing on AI’s potential to optimize operations, reduce costs, and boost revenue.

As AI continues to evolve, platforms like [Ema](https://ema.co) are offering even greater opportunities for financial institutions to automate workflows, improve efficiency, and ensure compliance, all while minimizing human error. For decision-makers in the financial sector, now is the time to embrace AI’s capabilities and harness its potential to drive long-term growth, operational excellence, and customer satisfaction.

[Hire Ema today](https://www.ema.co/hire-ema) to unlock the future of financial services with AI-driven innovation, efficiency, and seamless integration across your operations.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1. How is AI improving customer service in financial institutions?  
AI-powered chatbots and virtual assistants, such as Bank of America’s Erica, are helping banks scale customer support by automating basic queries and offering personalized experiences. These AI tools improve response times, reduce operational costs, and enhance customer satisfaction.

2. What impact does AI have on fraud detection and prevention?  
AI’s ability to analyze large datasets in real-time allows financial institutions to detect and prevent fraudulent activities more accurately. For instance, HSBC has reported a 50% reduction in false positives in its fraud detection system by using AI, which not only improves efficiency but also enhances trust with customers.

3. Can AI in financial services promote more inclusive lending practices?  
Yes, AI is helping make credit assessments more inclusive. Zest AI, for example, has helped lenders approve more loans for underrepresented groups, such as Latinos, women, and Black applicants, while maintaining consistent risk levels. AI models help lenders consider a broader range of data, improving loan access for diverse populations.

4. What role does AI play in wealth and asset management?  
AI is revolutionizing wealth management by enhancing portfolio optimization, risk management, and personalized investment strategies. Platforms like BlackRock’s Aladdin leverage AI to manage trillions of dollars in assets, ensuring more precise decision-making and providing predictive insights for fund managers.

5. How does Ema enhance AI capabilities in financial services?  
Ema, a Universal AI employee, automates complex workflows across various financial services functions. From automating loan approvals to managing customer support and ensuring compliance, Ema helps institutions streamline operations, reduce costs, and improve overall productivity without requiring additional human resources.

6. What are the long-term benefits of AI adoption in financial services?  
AI is expected to contribute to long-term growth in financial services by increasing revenue, reducing operational costs, and enhancing operational efficiency. It allows for faster decision-making, improved risk management, and more personalized services that cater to customer needs, providing a competitive advantage in an evolving market.

--- SOURCE 4: How much does AI save a company? ---
URL: https://www.randgroup.com/insights/services/ai-machine-learning/how-much-does-ai-save-a-company/

SUMMARY:
As AI continues to evolve, leading ERP and CRM platforms such as NetSuite and Microsoft Dynamics 365 are incorporating powerful AI tools to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and improve customer experiences. These integrations help businesses automate routine tasks, boost efficiency, and unlock data-driven insights, making AI a crucial asset in today’s competitive landscape. Key AI features in ERP and CRM platforms include automated invoice processing to reduce manual tasks and errors, AI-powered business intelligence for smarter and faster decision-making, predictive analytics to forecast trends and optimize operations, and AI-driven customer assistance for delivering personalized experiences. * **AI in Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations**: With Copilot, Dynamics 365 F&O users can streamline supply chain and finance operations by automating data management, forecasting issues, and optimizing procurement decisions.

# How much does AI save a company?

By [Heather Harris](https://www.randgroup.com/insights/author/hharris/ "Posts by Heather Harris") on September 20, 2024

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming business operations by driving significant cost savings and enhancing efficiency. Companies are increasingly adopting AI to automate tasks, optimize processes, and leverage data insights. But exactly how much does AI save a company? AI-driven solutions offer measurable benefits, including a 40% increase in productivity, a 40% reduction in costs, and a return on investment (ROI) within 6 to 12 months.

In this blog, we break down the time, money, and ROI savings that AI offers, showing its profound impact on business operations.

## What is AI?

Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the capability of machines to imitate intelligent human behavior. It encompasses a range of technologies designed to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as problem-solving, pattern recognition, and decision-making. By simulating human intelligence, AI enables machines to learn, reason, and perform tasks autonomously. This capability ranges from automating repetitive tasks to analyzing large datasets and generating predictions, making AI a core tool for enhancing business performance. Key benefits of AI include:

%

### increase in productivity

%

### decrease in costs

### months ROI

Across various sectors, AI offers numerous advantages. One of the key benefits is automation, as AI handles repetitive tasks, allowing human resources to focus on more complex activities. This leads to increased efficiency and a reduction in operational costs. AI also enhances decision-making by analyzing vast amounts of data to provide valuable insights and predictions, which helps businesses make more informed choices. Additionally, AI improves customer experience through tools such as chatbots and recommendation systems that enhance interactions and personalize services. Finally, AI fosters innovation by enabling the development of new products and services while also improving existing ones.

### Unleash the power of AI today

Ready to step into the future of business management? Contact us to learn how to unleash the power of AI and elevate your business to new heights of efficiency and intelligence.

[Contact us](/contact/)

## How much time does AI save a company?

%

### increase in efficiency

%

### increase in quality

%

### faster decision making

The Harvard Business Review reports that organizations using AI have seen productivity increases of up to 40% (Harvard Business Review, 2021). Additionally, AI can boost efficiency by 40%, improve the quality of output by 48%, and enable decisions to be made 44% faster. These benefits are achieved by automating repetitive tasks, analyzing vast amounts of data quickly, and providing real-time insights to enhance decision-making.

AI enhances productivity by automating routine tasks like data entry, scheduling, and reporting, which reduces the manual workload for employees. This frees up valuable time for teams to focus on more strategic, high-impact activities, boosting overall operational efficiency. AI tools also ensure consistency and accuracy, reducing human error and improving output quality across various departments.

Additionally, AI’s ability to rapidly analyze large datasets enables faster and more informed decision-making. By providing real-time insights and predictive analytics, companies can respond more quickly to market changes and customer demands. This improved decision-making process saves time and allows organizations to be more agile in a competitive environment.

## How much money does AI save a company?

%

### savings in operational costs

%

### savings in labor costs

%

### increase in revenue

A study by the McKinsey Global Institute estimates that AI can deliver cost reductions of up to 40% across various sectors by automating tasks and improving efficiency (McKinsey Global Institute, 2023). Additionally, AI can lead to 20% savings in operational costs, 30% savings in labor costs, and a 5-10% increase in revenue. These financial benefits stem from AI’s ability to optimize processes, reduce waste, and drive more efficient use of resources.

AI plays a key role in reducing operational costs by automating workflows and optimizing resource allocation. Through predictive maintenance, energy optimization, and real-time system monitoring, businesses can reduce waste and improve the efficiency of their processes. These improvements result in significant cost savings, allowing companies to operate leaner while maintaining high levels of productivity.

On the labor side, AI handles repetitive tasks like data processing, customer service automation, and report generation, freeing up employees to focus on strategic and creative initiatives. This shift not only reduces labor costs but also boosts employee satisfaction and performance. Moreover, AI-driven insights enable businesses to target customers more effectively, improving engagement and driving increased revenue by helping companies unlock new growth opportunities.

## What is the ROI of AI?

%

### marketing ROI

%

### increase in sales productivity

%

### increase in customer satisfaction

Leaders surveyed in a Deloitte report expect a substantial transformation in less than twelve months (Deloitte, 2024). Additionally, AI can deliver a 38% ROI in marketing efforts, a 15% boost in sales productivity, and a 20% increase in customer satisfaction, often achieving a full return on investment within 6 to 12 months. These returns are driven by AI’s ability to optimize marketing strategies, enhance sales operations, and improve the overall customer experience.

AI-powered tools help marketing teams target customers more accurately and automate campaigns, leading to higher engagement and better conversion rates. This efficiency translates into a substantial marketing ROI as businesses can make smarter decisions about where and how to allocate resources. In sales, AI enables more efficient lead generation, customer insights, and sales forecasting, all of which increase sales productivity by freeing up time for sales teams to focus on closing deals and building stronger client relationships.

Customer satisfaction also improves as AI provides personalized experiences through predictive analytics and tailored solutions. By anticipating customer needs and resolving issues more efficiently, businesses can boost loyalty and retention. These combined benefits result in AI’s ability to pay for itself relatively quickly, with many companies seeing a complete return on investment within a 6 to 12-month timeframe.

In order to achieve the full return on investment (ROI) from AI, it’s crucial that these solutions are implemented correctly and optimized for your specific business processes. Studies show that [55% to 75% of software implementation projects fail](/insights/services/solution-implementation/what-percentage-of-erp-implementations-fail/), often due to poor execution or a lack of alignment with company needs. To avoid this, selecting the right implementation partner is essential. A skilled partner can ensure that the AI and software solutions are tailored to your workflows and deliver the anticipated benefits in efficiency, productivity, and customer satisfaction.

## AI integration into ERP and CRM systems

As AI continues to evolve, leading ERP and CRM platforms such as [NetSuite](/oracle-netsuite/netsuite/) and [Microsoft Dynamics 365](/microsoft/dynamics-365/) are incorporating powerful AI tools to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and improve customer experiences. These integrations help businesses automate routine tasks, boost efficiency, and unlock data-driven insights, making AI a crucial asset in today’s competitive landscape. Both NetSuite and [Microsoft Dynamics 365 Copilot](/microsoft/dynamics-365/copilot/) offer sophisticated AI capabilities to transform how companies manage their workflows and deliver value.

Key AI features in ERP and CRM platforms include automated invoice processing to reduce manual tasks and errors, AI-powered business intelligence for smarter and faster decision-making, predictive analytics to forecast trends and optimize operations, and AI-driven customer assistance for delivering personalized experiences. Additionally, seamless AI integration into familiar tools like Outlook and Teams enhances overall productivity and user experience.

* **AI in NetSuite**: NetSuite’s AI automates routine tasks like invoice processing, improves financial accuracy, and offers predictive analytics for better decision-making. [Read more about AI in NetSuite.](/insights/oracle-netsuite/discover-the-powerful-ai-capabilities-embedded-in-netsuite/)
* **Dynamics 365 Business Central with Copilot**: Copilot enhances Business Central by automating bank reconciliations, offering conversational AI assistance, and improving inventory management. [Discover AI in Dynamics 365 Business Central.](/insights/microsoft/dynamics-365/business-central/drive-business-efficiency-with-ai-in-dynamics-365-business-central/)
* **AI in Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations**: With Copilot, Dynamics 365 F&O users can streamline supply chain and finance operations by automating data management, forecasting issues, and optimizing procurement decisions. [Learn more about AI in Dynamics 365 F&O.](/insights/microsoft/dynamics-365/finance/harnessing-the-power-of-ai-in-dynamics-365-finance-and-operations/)
* **AI in Dynamics 365 Sales**: Copilot helps sales teams by summarizing records, assisting with email communication, and providing natural language search features, all to boost sales effectiveness. [Explore AI in Dynamics 365 Sales.](/insights/microsoft/dynamics-365/sales/leverage-the-power-of-ai-with-copilot-in-dynamics-365-sales/)
* **AI in Dynamics 365 Customer Service**: AI assists customer service agents with real-time case summaries, automated responses, and optimized chat experiences for faster resolution times. [See how AI enhances Dynamics 365 Customer Service.](/insights/microsoft/dynamics-365/customer-service/discover-how-ai-in-dynamics-365-customer-service-revolutionizes-customer-experiences/)
* **AI in Dynamics 365 Field Service**: Copilot aids field service operations with natural language processing for work orders, real-time updates, and intelligent resource scheduling. [Uncover AI’s impact on Dynamics 365 Field Service.](/insights/microsoft/dynamics-365/field-service/revolutionize-dynamics-365-field-service-with-ai-powered-copilot/)

## Rand Group’s software & AI services

At Rand Group, we specialize in delivering tailored ERP and CRM solutions using advanced AI technology to drive efficiency and business success. Our expertise includes comprehensive support for platforms such as Microsoft Dynamics 365 and NetSuite, providing customized implementations, expert consulting, and ongoing support to meet your specific needs. By [integrating AI](/software-engineering/ai-machine-learning/), we enhance predictive analytics, automate processes, and deliver advanced reporting to help you make informed decisions and optimize performance.

* **System optimization**: We optimize your existing software to ensure maximum performance, leveraging AI to automate processes and improve decision-making.
* **Ongoing support & maintenance**: We provide [continuous support](/support/) to keep your systems running smoothly, ensuring that your technology remains up-to-date and efficient.

## Next steps

AI is a powerful tool that offers considerable financial benefits by enhancing efficiency, reducing costs, and driving innovation. As AI technology continues to evolve, its potential for saving money and improving business performance will only grow. Embracing AI can provide your company with a competitive edge and significant cost advantages, making it an essential investment for the future.

The integration of AI into ERP and CRM systems by providers like NetSuite and Microsoft is transforming how businesses operate. By harnessing the power of AI, these platforms offer advanced capabilities that improve efficiency, drive insightful decision-making, and enhance customer engagement. To learn more, [contact us today.](/contact/)

What is AI in finance?
----------------------

Artificial intelligence (AI) in finance is the use of technology, including advanced algorithms and machine learning (ML), to analyze data, automate tasks and improve decision-making in the financial services industry.

[Artificial intelligence](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/artificial-intelligence)in finance refers to the application of a set of technologies, particularly [machine learning](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/machine-learning) algorithms, in the finance industry. This fintech enables financial services organizations to improve the efficiency, accuracy and speed of such tasks as data analytics, forecasting, investment management, risk management, fraud detection, customer service and more.

AI is modernizing the financial industry by automating traditionally manual banking processes, enabling a better understanding of financial markets and creating ways to engage customers that mimic human intelligence and interaction.

AI is revolutionizing how financial institutions operate and fueling startups. AI models run trades with remarkable speed and precision, taking advantage of real-time market data to unlock deeper insights and dictate where investments are made. By analyzing intricate patterns in transaction datasets, AI solutions allow financial organizations to improve [risk management](https://www.ibm.com/topics/risk-management), which includes security, fraud, anti-money laundering (AML), know your customer (KYC) and compliance initiatives.

AI is also changing the way financial organizations engage with customers, predicting their behavior and understanding their purchase preferences. This prediction enables more personalized interactions, faster and more accurate customer support, credit scoring refinements and innovative products and services.

Overall, the integration of AI in finance is creating a new era of data-driven decision-making, efficiency, security and customer experience in the financial sector.

![Image 1: Black woman working on laptop](https://www.ibm.com/content/dam/connectedassets-adobe-cms/worldwide-content/stock-assets/getty/image/photography/d3/03/pi-2778437.component.think-ad-xl.ts=1746031261570.jpg/content/experience-fragments/adobe-cms/us/en/site-v2/think-hub/article/_8_column_general_ad/blueprint---think-ad---xf---do-not-modify/thinkad-article-generalnewsletter/_jcr_content/root/think_ad_copy/image)

### Stay ahead of the latest tech news

Get weekly insights, research and expert views on AI, security, cloud and more in the Think Newsletter.

[Subscribe today](https://www.ibm.com/account/reg/signup?formid=news-urx-52954)

How is AI used in finance?
--------------------------

Here are some key areas where AI is commonly applied in the financial industry:

**Algorithmic trading:**AI can be used to develop trading algorithms that can analyze market trends and historical data to decide and perform trades faster than humans.

**Automation and efficiency:**AI can automate repetitive and time-consuming tasks, allowing financial institutions to process large amounts of data faster and more accurately.

**Competitive advantage:**AI can help financial institutions foster innovation and stay at the forefront of technology, which can give them a competitive edge.

**Compliance:**AI can automate monitoring and reporting requirements to ensure regulatory compliance

**Credit scoring:**AI can analyze various data, including social media activity and other online behavior, to assess customers’ creditworthiness and make more accurate credit decisions.

**Cost reduction:**By automating tasks, financial institutions can reduce manual labor, streamline workflows and improve operational efficiency, which can reduce costs.

**Customer service:**By answering questions and completing routine tasks 24/7, AI-powered personal assistants and [chatbots](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/chatbots) can reduce the need for human intervention, provide personalized customer service such as real-time credit approvals and offer consumers improved fraud protection and cybersecurity.

**Data analysis:**AI can analyze massive amounts of data and extract insights and trends that would be difficult for human data scientists to detect, enabling more informed decision-making and a deeper understanding of market behavior.

**Fraud detection:**AI algorithms can prevent financial crime, such as fraud and cyberattacks, by identifying unusual patterns in financial transactions. This approach helps improve security in activities such as online banking and credit card transactions.

**Loan processing:**AI can better predict and assess loan risks, and streamline the process and approvals for borrowers by automating tasks such as risk assessment, credit scoring and document verification.

**Personal finances:**AI tools can help people manage their personal finances by analyzing goals, spending patterns and risk tolerance to develop budgeting advice and savings strategies.

**Portfolio management:**AI can analyze market conditions and economic indicators to help investors make better decisions and optimize their portfolios.

**Predictive analytics:**AI can enable predictive modeling, which can help financial organizations anticipate market trends, potential risks and customer behavior.

**Risk management:**AI can analyze data to help financial organizations assess and manage risks more effectively and create a more secure and stable financial environment.

**Sentiment analysis:**AI can analyze news sources, social media and other information to gauge market sentiment, which can help predict market trends and influence decision-making.

 AI Academy 

### Put AI to work for finance

Generative AI is completely revolutionizing the role of finance. Learn how the adoption of AI is helping CFOs and finance teams find new ways of making the seemingly impossible, possible.

[Go to episode](https://www.ibm.com/think/videos/ai-academy/ai-finance)

Key stakeholders of AI in finance
---------------------------------

A diverse set of stakeholders implement, operate, regulate and use AI technologies in the financial sector. These participants include:

**Auditors and internal control teams:**Responsible for assessing the effectiveness of AI systems, these individuals and groups conduct audits to identify potential issues and risks and ensure efficiency, accuracy and compliance.

**Chief information officers (CIOs) and chief technology officers (CTOs):**As overseers of the organization’s technical infrastructure, CIOs and CTOs make key decisions regarding AI implementation, usage and security.

**Customers:**A positive user experience with AI-driven apps is necessary for customers and end users to have confidence and trust in the financial organization.

**Developers:**AI developers are responsible for designing and implementing AI systems into the organization, and ensuring their accuracy and effectiveness.

**Ethics and diversity officers:**Organizations task these individuals with guarding against bias, ensuring fairness and inclusivity in the use of AI.

**Executives**: Top executives and the board of directors make strategic decisions regarding the implementation and use of AI initiatives and their proper management.

**Financial organizations**: Banks, investment firms and other financial institutions implement AI to increase the effectiveness of fraud detection, risk management, underwriting, investment strategies and customer service.

**Legal teams:**These teams work with regulators to ensure that AI applications comply with relevant laws and industry regulations.

**Risk management teams:**As AI is often used for assessing and mitigating risk in financial organizations, these teams monitor the effectiveness of the AI systems.

Governance of AI in finance
---------------------------

The use of AI in finance requires monitoring to ensure proper use and minimal risk. Proactive governance can drive responsible, ethical and transparent AI usage, which is critical as financial institutions handle vast amounts of sensitive data.

Guardrails to ensure ethics, regulatory compliance, transparency and explainability—so that stakeholders understand the decisions made by the financial institution—are essential in order to balance the benefits of AI with responsible and accountable use. By establishing oversight and clear rules regarding its application, AI can continue to evolve as a trusted, powerful tool in the financial industry.

Use cases of AI in finance
--------------------------

Various types of financial institutions leverage AI to improve efficiency, decision-making and user experience (UX). Some examples of AI in finance include:

**Customer service:**[Conversational AI](https://research.ibm.com/topics/conversational-ai) and [natural language processing](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/natural-language-processing) (NLP) power chatbots that allow banking customers to access account information quickly and efficiently, 24/7.

**Cyberattack prevention:**AI can use [data science](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-science) to analyze patterns and trends and alert companies to unusual activity.

**Financial planning:**Robo-advisors use sophisticated algorithms to provide affordable, personalized investment advice based on customers’ goals, risk tolerance and market conditions.

**Fraud detection and prevention:**Deep learning can be used to analyze customers’ buying behavior and trigger an alert when unusual spending patterns are identified.

**Loan eligibility:**Lenders are managing risk by deploying AI neural networks to quickly analyze data to determine customers’ creditworthiness.

**Trading:**Investment firms use AI for algorithmic trading—trades made at high-speed based on real-time data and market trends.

enriched_points.expanded_content: AI streamlines operations by automating repetitive tasks, enabling financial teams to focus on strategic initiatives, thus enhancing overall productivity and operational efficiency.
Through automation of manual processes, AI significantly reduces operational costs and minimizes errors, leading to improved efficiency and higher profitability for financial institutions.
AI analyzes vast datasets to provide real-time insights, enabling financial institutions to make informed, data-driven decisions, improving risk management and strategic planning.
AI-driven customer service solutions, such as chatbots, provide personalized support, reducing response times and enhancing the overall customer experience in financial transactions.
By adopting AI, financial institutions can innovate faster and improve service delivery, positioning themselves ahead of competitors in an increasingly digital and data-driven market.
